title: Winey Irish Lamb Braise
description: A hearty and flavorful Irish dish featuring lamb, dried mint, and a rich wine sauce. Perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon.

ingredients.name: red wine
ingredients.notes: such as Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: such as kosher or sea salt

instructions: In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, heat 1 tablespoon of butter over medium-high heat. Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter to the pot and cook until melted. Add the onion, carrots, and celery and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the garlic, dried mint, salt, and pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the red wine to the pot and cook until reduced by half, about 5 minutes, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the beef broth, brown sugar (if using), and browned lamb to the pot. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven. Braise the lamb for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until tender and falling apart.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly. Serve the lamb and sauce hot, garnished with fresh mint leaves if desired.
Make sure to skim off any excess fat from the sauce before serving.
Serve the Winey Irish Lamb Braise with mashed potatoes, crusty bread, or over egg noodles.
Note: If you prefer a thicker sauce, you can reduce the amount of beef broth or add a little cornstarch or flour to thicken.
To make the dish ahead of time, cook the lamb and sauce as directed, then refrigerate or freeze until ready to serve.
To reheat, simmer the lamb and sauce over low heat until warmed through.
